title: Athlete consent — sample scenarios
description: Reference examples of consent snapshots + recording flows for the tenant admin UI's onboarding tour + the /athletes/{athlete}/consents playground. Every sample is anonymised — no real user IDs. Real-world consent recording flows use the athlete's live guardian roster.

samples.name: Minor — full consent
samples.description: The typical case for a 12-year-old whose parent has recorded every consent at signup.

samples.name: Minor — photo declined
samples.description: Parent opts the child out of marketing photos but agrees to medical disclosure to coaches (safety) + third-party sharing (insurance).

samples.name: Minor — medical declined (bare minimum)
samples.description: Highly privacy-conscious parent — only third-party sharing agreed to (mandatory for insurance-covered activity).
samples.notes: In this scenario, coaches DO NOT see medical fields even if they're recorded — the MedicalDisclosureGate returns false. HR staff still see them.

samples.name: Adult — self-recorded
samples.description: An 22-year-old adult athlete recording their own consents at signup.

samples.name: Adult — turned 18 mid-year (re-consent flow)
samples.description: Athlete just crossed the legal_adult_age boundary. Prior guardian-recorded consents expired; the athlete must re-record their own.
samples.notes: AthleteMinorTurnedAdult fires on the 18th birthday; the SDUI surfaces a re-consent banner + walks the newly-adult athlete through recording their own snapshot.

samples.name: Expired snapshot (workflow entitlement on)
samples.description: Consents recorded 380 days ago — past the 365-day re-consent cadence. Tenant carries athlete_consent_workflow entitlement. Athlete is flagged as expired; downstream photo-consent-required flows refuse until re-consent.

samples.name: Revoked photo consent
samples.description: Parent revoked photo release after the child's photo appeared in a public gallery they didn't remember approving. Fires AthleteConsentRevoked; marketing team notified to remove existing images.

revocation_history.reason: Photo appeared in marketing without explicit approval
